questions.text: All of the following are common molecules in membranes of all type of cells except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Globular proteins, also known as globular or soluble proteins, are three-dimensional, compactly folded proteins that are soluble in water. These proteins perform a wide range of functions within cells, such as enzymatic activities, signal transduction, transport of molecules, and structural support. Globular proteins are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell membranes. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Cholesterol is a type of lipid that is a major component of eukaryotic cell membranes. It helps maintain membrane fluidity and stability by interacting with phospholipids. Cholesterol is present in large amounts (around 20-40 mol%) in eukaryotic plasma membranes, but it is universally absent in the membranes of prokaryotes. Therefore, this option is correct as cholesterol is present in eukaryotic cell membranes but not in the prokaryotic cell membranes.

Carbohydrates are commonly found in cell membranes of both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells in the form of glycoproteins and glycolipids. These carbohydrates play important roles in cell-cell recognition, cell adhesion, and other cellular functions. Hence, this option is also incorrect.

This option is incorrect because phospholipids are a fundamental component of all cell membranes, both prokaryotic and eukaryotic. They are amphipathic molecules, meaning they have both hydrophilic (polar) and hydrophobic (nonpolar) regions. Phospholipids spontaneously arrange themselves in a bilayer structure with their hydrophobic tails facing inward and hydrophilic heads facing outward, creating the basic framework of the cell membrane.

questions.explanation: Cholesterol is a type of lipid that is a major component of eukaryotic cell membranes. It helps maintain membrane fluidity and stability by interacting with phospholipids. Cholesterol is present in large amounts (around 20-40 mol%) in eukaryotic plasma membranes, but it is universally absent in the membranes of prokaryotes. Therefore, the correct option is cholesterol as it is not present in all types of cell membranes.

questions.text: Hydrophobic portion of plasma membrane is present in/at:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The extracellular matrix of plasma membrane is surrounded by water molecules. So, hydrophobic portions will not be present towards extracellular matrix.
The cyroplasm mainly contains water so hydrophibic portions cannot be present towards cytoplasm.

The outside of plasma membrane is surrounded by water molecules. The hydrophobic portions will be present away from water moelcules i.e., towards the inner core of plasma membrane.(Hydrophobic means water-repelling)
Hydrophobic portions are not present towards cytoskeleton.

questions.explanation: The outside of plasma membrane is surrounded by water molecules. The hydrophobic portions will be present away from water moelcules i.e., towards the inner core of plasma membrane. (Hydrophobic means water-repelling)

questions.text: In plasma membrane, carbohydrates combine with the lipids and proteins to form glycolipids and glycoproteins and are oriented:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option suggests that glycolipids and glycoproteins are oriented towards the interior of the plasma membrane. However, in reality, they are positioned on the outer surface, making this option incorrect.

This is the correct option. Glycolipids and glycoproteins are indeed oriented towards the outer surface of the plasma membrane, forming a protective layer known as the glycocalyx and serving important roles as receptors.

This option implies a dual orientation of glycolipids and glycoproteins, which is not accurate. They are specifically oriented towards the outer surface of the plasma membrane, making this option incorrect.

This option suggests a random arrangement of glycolipids and glycoproteins, which contradicts their specific orientation towards the outer surface of the plasma membrane, thus rendering this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that glycolipids and glycoproteins are oriented towards the outside of the plasma membrane. This arrangement allows them to participate in the formation of the glycocalyx, which serves as a protective layer and plays essential roles in cell recognition and signaling. The other options are incorrect because they misrepresent the specific orientation of these molecules. Glycolipids and glycoproteins are not found towards the inside (Option A), do not have a dual orientation (Option C), and are not randomly distributed (Option D). Their strategic placement on the outer surface is critical for their function in cellular interactions.

questions.text: Which of the following acts as receptor site on plasma membrane?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The head of phospholipid molecules is hydrophilic and contributes to the structure of the plasma membrane, but it does not function as a receptor site.
The tails of phospholipid molecules are hydrophobic and help to create the membrane's barrier, but they do not serve as receptor sites.
Cholesterol molecules help to maintain membrane fluidity and stability, but they do not act as receptor sites for signaling molecules.
Glycoproteins are integral to the plasma membrane structure and serve as receptor sites for hormones and other signaling molecules, making them crucial for cellular communication.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Glycoprotein. Glycoproteins, which are proteins with carbohydrate chains attached, are embedded in the plasma membrane and play a key role as receptor sites. They can recognize and bind to various signaling molecules, including hormones, which initiates cellular responses.

questions.text: Which of the following function is performed by both types of endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is primarily responsible for protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface. The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) does not participate in protein synthesis as it lacks ribosomes.

This option is correct. Both the RER and the SER provide mechanical support to the cell by forming a structural network. This network helps maintain the cell's shape and stabilize its cytoplasm. Additionally, they contribute to the overall structural integrity of the cell.

This option is incorrect. Muscle contraction involves calcium ions, which are stored in the SER. The RER does not play a role in muscle contraction, as it is not involved in calcium storage or release.

This option is incorrect. Detoxification is primarily a function of the SER, which contains enzymes like cytochrome P450 to metabolize drugs. The RER does not participate in the detoxification process.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that both types of endoplasmic reticulum (RER and SER) provide mechanical support to the cell. They form a network of membranes that help maintain the structural integrity of the cell. The RER is primarily involved in protein synthesis, while the SER is involved in lipid synthesis, calcium storage, and detoxification of drugs. However, neither protein synthesis nor detoxification is a function shared by both types of endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.text: Which of the following organelles is responsible for the production of steroid hormones?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Lysosomes are primarily involved in the breakdown of cellular waste materials and macromolecules, not in the synthesis of steroid hormones. They contain digestive enzymes that degrade proteins, lipids, and other macromolecules.

This option is incorrect. The Golgi bodies, or Golgi apparatus, are involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids for secretion or use within the cell. They are not directly responsible for the production of steroid hormones.

This option is correct. The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is the organelle responsible for the synthesis of steroid hormones. It contains enzymes required for the synthesis and modification of lipids, including cholesterol, which is the precursor for steroid hormone production.

This option is incorrect. The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is primarily involved in the synthesis of proteins destined for secretion or for use in the cell membrane. It is not involved in the synthesis of steroid hormones.

questions.explanation: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is crucial for the synthesis of steroid hormones, as it harbors enzymes necessary for the production of cholesterol and its conversion into various steroid hormones. This function is distinct from that of other organelles: lysosomes are involved in the degradation of cellular debris, the Golgi apparatus modifies and packages proteins and lipids, and the rough endoplasmic reticulum is dedicated to protein synthesis. This specialized role makes the SER essential in steroidogenic cells like those found in the adrenal glands and ovaries.

questions.text: Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) contains 40.92% carbon, 4.58% hydrogen and 54.5% of oxygen by mass. What is the empirical formula of ascorbic acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct option is (a) C3H4O3, as it matches the empirical formula derived from the given percentages of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.
This formula does not have the same ratios as the given percentages. It suggests a higher proportion of oxygen compared to the given data.
This formula suggests only one carbon atom, which is not in agreement with the given percentage of carbon.
This formula suggests six carbon atoms, which is not consistent with the given data.
This formula suggests two carbon atoms, which does not match the given percentage of carbon.

questions.explanation: The empirical formula is the ratio between the number of atoms of different elements which is present in the molecule of the given compound. Empirical formulas usually give the proportion of the elements present in the molecule of the given compound but not the actual number of the atoms or how the atoms are arranged. Complete step by step answer: If the mass of the compound is given then the empirical formula is calculated by changing the mass of every element to moles and then by dividing every mole value by the lowest value of mole computed and then the value of mole is rounded off to the closest whole number. Given in the question: 100% of vitamin C contains 40.92% carbon by mass, 4.58% hydrogen, and 54.50% oxygen Or we can say that 100 g of vitamin C contains 40.92 g carbon by mass, 4.58 g hydrogen, and 54.50 g oxygen. The atomic mass of carbon = 12 g/mol The atomic mass of hydrogen = 1 g/mol The atomic mass of oxygen = 16 g/mol The number of moles is calculated by the ratio of the given mass to the molar mass. Number of moles of carbon = 40.92\12 = 3.4 mole Number of moles of hydrogen = 4.58\1 = 4.58 mole Number of moles of oxygen = 54.50\16= 3.40 mole The molar ratio of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen is: C:H:O 3.4 : 4.58 : 3.4 = 3:4:3 C3H4O3 So, the correct answer is “Option A”. Note: If the molecular formula of a compound is given then to calculate the empirical formula from the molecular formula we have to divide all the subscripts in the empirical formula by the simplest whole number. The molecular formula is the actual whole number ratio of the elements present in the molecule of the compound.

questions.text: Choose the correct antonym of the given word:Eradication
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: To hold back something by force means to suppress.
Termination is the complete destruction of something. It is a synonym of eradication
Control is the power to influence or direct people's behaviour or the course of events
Establish is the correct antonym of eradication as establish means to create or set up while eradication means extinction or termination.

questions.explanation: To establish something means to begin it or bring it about. It means the complete opposite of eradication.

questions.text: 2 grams of H2 molecules contain _ molecules.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. This value suggests twice the number of molecules in 2 grams of H2, which is not correct as only 1 mole of H2 is present.
This is the correct answer. 2 grams of H2 equals 1 mole, which contains Avogadro's number of molecules, 6.022 x 1023.
This option is incorrect. This value represents half of Avogadro's number, which would correspond to half a mole of H2, not the full 2 grams.
This option is incorrect. This value is the atomic mass of hydrogen in atomic mass units, not the number of molecules.
This option is incorrect. This number could suggest 2 molecules, but the question asks for the total number of molecules in 2 grams of H2.

questions.explanation: 1 mole of hydrogen contains = 2×1 = 2g2g of H2 contain = 2/2 × avogadro number = 1 × 6.022 × 10²³ = 6.022 × 10²³ molecules.

questions.text: Cellular organelles with membranes are
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are non-membrane-bound organelles made of ribosomal RNA and proteins. They serve as the site of protein synthesis in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

Lysosomes and the Golgi apparatus are enclosed by single membranes, while mitochondria have a double membrane. These membranes help compartmentalize functions like digestion, protein processing, and energy production.

Ribosomes lack a surrounding membrane and float freely in the cytoplasm or on the rough ER. In contrast, nuclei and mitochondria are membrane-bound, which helps compartmentalize their specialized functions.

Ribosomes are non-membrane-bound structures that synthesize proteins. Chromosomes, on the other hand, are composed of DNA and proteins and are located within the nucleus, not as independent organelles.

questions.explanation: These organelles are membrane-bound: lysosomes and the Golgi apparatus have single membranes, while mitochondria have double membranes.

questions.text: The osmotic expansion of a cell kept in water is chiefly regulated by
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria generate ATP by cellular respiration, providing energy for the cell. They do not regulate osmotic balance, which is mainly managed by the plasma membrane and vacuoles.
Vacuoles are enclosed by a membrane and store water, nutrients, and waste. They help maintain osmotic balance and turgor pressure, supporting the cell’s structure and proper functioning.

Plastids, such as chloroplasts and amyloplasts, are responsible for photosynthesis and storing substances like starch. They do not directly regulate osmotic balance, which is mainly controlled by vacuoles and the plasma membrane.
Ribosomes synthesize proteins by translating mRNA but do not participate in water regulation or maintaining osmotic balance, which is handled by structures like vacuoles and the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles that store nutrients and waste products, helping maintain osmotic balance and cell turgor by controlling water uptake.

questions.text: The Golgi complex plays a major role
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids but does not generate energy. Energy production is primarily carried out by mitochondria through cellular respiration.

options.text: In post-translational modification of proteins and glycosylation of lipids
options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus is essential for processing and modifying proteins after synthesis, including adding sugar groups to proteins and lipids. These modifications ensure proteins are functional and correctly targeted within or outside the cell.

options.text: In capturing light energy and converting it into chemical energy
options.explanation: Light energy is captured by chlorophyll in chloroplasts and converted into chemical energy through photosynthesis. The Golgi complex does not participate in energy capture or conversion; its role is in processing and packaging cellular molecules.

options.text: In the intracellular digestion of proteins and carbohydrates
options.explanation: Lysosomes carry out intracellular digestion by breaking down macromolecules, old organelles, and cellular debris using hydrolytic enzymes. The Golgi complex, in contrast, mainly modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids.

questions.explanation: The Golgi complex is integral to the post-translational modification of proteins and the glycosylation of lipids. Proteins synthesized in the rough endoplasmic reticulum and lipids from the smooth endoplasmic reticulum are further processed in the Golgi apparatus. Here, proteins undergo modifications such as folding and cutting, and both proteins and lipids are glycosylated, meaning they are combined with carbohydrates to form glycoproteins and glycolipids, respectively. This is essential for their proper function and transport within the cell.

questions.text: Nucleoproteins are synthesised in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nucleoplasm is the semi-fluid substance within the nucleus where various vital processes occur, such as DNA replication and RNA transcription. However, the initial synthesis of proteins, including nucleoproteins, predominantly takes place in the cytoplasm before these proteins are transported to the nucleus.

The nuclear envelope serves as a barrier that encases the nucleus and regulates the exchange of substances between the nucleus and the cytoplasm. It does not serve as the site for the synthesis of proteins, including nucleoproteins.

The nucleolus is known for ribosome production, specifically the assembly of rRNA and ribosomal proteins into ribosomal subunits. Although it plays a significant role in the production of rRNA components of ribosomes, it is not the primary site for the synthesis of nucleoproteins as a whole.

This is the correct option. The cytoplasm is where the majority of protein synthesis occurs, including the synthesis of nucleoproteins. Once synthesized, these proteins may be transported to the nucleus or other cellular locations where they perform important functions related to genetic material.

questions.explanation: The cytoplasm is the primary site for protein synthesis in the cell, including the synthesis of nucleoproteins, which are complexes of proteins and nucleic acids. These proteins are synthesized in the cytoplasm and then transported to their functional locations within the cell, such as the nucleus. The nucleoplasm is involved in processes like DNA replication and RNA synthesis but not in the initial synthesis of proteins. The nuclear envelope acts as a boundary and mediator for transport, while the nucleolus specializes in ribosome assembly, not the synthesis of nucleoproteins.

questions.text: What is an example of a semi autonomous organelle found in both animal and plant cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum is not a semi-autonomous organelle as it does not have its own DNA.
Chloroplasts are semi-autonomous as they contain their own DNA, but they are found only in plant cells and not in animal cells.
The Golgi apparatus is involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins, but it is not semi-autonomous and lacks its own DNA.
Mitochondria are semi-autonomous organelles as they have their own DNA and are present in both plant and animal cells.

questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the correct answer because they are semi-autonomous organelles found in both animal and plant cells. They possess their own circular DNA and 70S ribosomes, allowing them to replicate independently within the cell. Chloroplasts also share these features, but they are exclusive to plant cells and some protists, not animal cells. The endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi apparatus do not have their own DNA and are not considered semi-autonomous.

questions.text: Which of the following are colorless?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chloroplasts are green due to chlorophyll, which is crucial for photosynthesis. They are not colorless.
Chromoplasts contain various pigments that provide different colors to plant parts, aiding in pollination. They are not colorless.
Leucoplasts are indeed colorless plastids focused on storing nutrients like starches, oils, and proteins. They lack pigmentation, distinguishing them from other plastid types.
This option is incorrect because among the choices, leucoplasts are the plastids that are colorless.

questions.explanation: Leucoplasts are colorless because they do not contain pigments. They serve primarily as storage organelles for nutrients like starch, oils, and proteins. Chloroplasts are green due to chlorophyll and are involved in photosynthesis, while chromoplasts contain pigments that give them various colors, aiding in attracting pollinators. Therefore, leucoplasts are the only plastids among the options given that are colorless.

questions.text: A group of ribosomes attached to messenger RNA is known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A ribosome is a cellular structure that synthesizes proteins by translating mRNA into polypeptide chains. However, it refers to a single unit, not a group of ribosomes working together.
A lysosome is an organelle responsible for breaking down waste materials and cellular debris. It is not involved in protein synthesis or translation.
A nucleosome is a unit of DNA packaging in eukaryotic cells, consisting of DNA wrapped around histone proteins. It plays a role in DNA organization and gene regulation, not in protein synthesis.
A polysome, or polyribosome, is a group of ribosomes attached to a single mRNA molecule, simultaneously translating it into protein. This is the correct term for the described structure.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Polysome. A polysome is a structure where multiple ribosomes are attached to a single mRNA strand, facilitating efficient protein synthesis by translating the mRNA simultaneously. This differs from a single ribosome, which works independently, and from structures like lysosomes and nucleosomes, which have entirely different functions unrelated to mRNA translation.

questions.text: What is the wall strenghtening material in the prokaryotic cell wall?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose is a major component of plant cell walls, providing structural support to plant cells, not prokaryotic cells.
Silica is found in some plant structures and diatoms but is not a component of prokaryotic cell walls.
Chitin is a component of fungal cell walls, providing strength and rigidity, but it is not found in prokaryotic cell walls.
Peptidoglycan is the primary strengthening material in the cell walls of most bacteria, providing structural integrity and shape.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is peptidoglycan. Bacterial cell walls are primarily composed of peptidoglycan, which is a polymer consisting of sugars and amino acids. It forms a mesh-like layer outside the plasma membrane, providing the cell with structural strength and rigidity. This is distinct from the cell walls of plants, which contain cellulose, and fungi, which contain chitin. Silica is not a component of prokaryotic cell walls.

questions.text: Plastids are only found in the :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. Plastids are not present in animals because they do not perform photosynthesis. Plastids are unique to plants and some algae, where they contribute to photosynthesis and storage.
Incorrect. Animals do not contain plastids as they are heterotrophic organisms that rely on external sources for energy, not photosynthesis.
Correct. Plastids are found in plants, where they are crucial for photosynthesis and the storage of vital compounds.
Incorrect. Viruses are acellular and lack organelles, including plastids, as they are not considered living organisms and cannot perform cellular functions.

questions.explanation: Plastids are specialized organelles found in plants and some algae, where they play vital roles in processes like photosynthesis and the storage of essential compounds. Animals do not possess plastids because they obtain their energy from consuming organic material rather than photosynthesis. Viruses, being acellular entities, do not contain any organelles, including plastids. Hence, the correct answer is that plastids are found only in plants.

questions.text: Which organelle synthesizes enzymes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitochondria generate energy for the cell through ATP synthesis and contain enzymes for metabolic processes, but they do not synthesize enzymes themselves.
Vacuoles are involved in storage and maintaining cellular homeostasis. They do not play a role in the synthesis of proteins or enzymes.

The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is involved in the folding and modification of proteins that are initially synthesized by ribosomes. While it aids in protein processing, it is not the site of initial enzyme synthesis.
Ribosomes are the cellular structures where protein synthesis occurs, including the synthesis of enzymes. They translate mRNA into polypeptide chains, which then fold into functional proteins.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the primary site where proteins, including enzymes, are synthesized. They read the genetic code carried by mRNA and translate it into a sequence of amino acids to form proteins. The rough endoplasmic reticulum provides a platform for further processing and folding of these proteins but does not synthesize them. Mitochondria are specialized for energy production and not enzyme synthesis, while vacuoles are primarily storage organelles and do not participate in protein synthesis.

questions.text: The model of plasma membrane suggests that proteins are embedded in lipid bilayer.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The unit membrane model proposes a static trilaminar structure with proteins on the surface, which does not accurately reflect the integrated and dynamic positioning of proteins within the membrane.

The fluid mosaic model accurately represents the plasma membrane as a dynamic and flexible structure, with proteins embedded within the phospholipid bilayer, allowing lateral movement and functional diversity.
While 'permeable' indicates a characteristic of the membrane allowing certain substances to pass through, it is not a model describing protein arrangement within the membrane.
An ultracentrifuge is a piece of laboratory equipment used for separating substances based on density, unrelated to any model of membrane structure.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the 'Fluid Mosaic' model, which describes the plasma membrane as a flexible and dynamic structure with proteins embedded within the phospholipid bilayer. This model accounts for the lateral movement of proteins, contributing to the membrane's fluidity and mosaic-like appearance. The 'Unit membrane' model fails to capture this dynamic nature. 'Permeable' is a property of membranes, not a model, and 'Ultracentrifuge' is a tool used in laboratory processes, not related to membrane structure.

questions.text: Lipid metabolism is the function of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes assemble amino acids into proteins by reading mRNA sequences. They are not involved in making or processing lipids, which occurs in organelles like the smooth endoplasmic reticulum. Their main function is producing the cell’s protein machinery.

The sarcoplasmic reticulum stores and releases calcium ions to control muscle contraction. It regulates calcium levels during contraction and relaxation cycles. Lipid synthesis is carried out by other organelles, like the smooth endoplasmic reticulum.

The Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum has ribosomes on its surface that translate proteins for secretion or membrane insertion. It processes and folds these proteins before sending them to the Golgi apparatus. Lipid synthesis is mainly handled by the smooth ER, not the RER.

The Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum produces lipids such as phospholipids and steroids for membranes and hormones. It also helps detoxify drugs and harmful substances in the cell. Unlike the rough ER, it lacks ribosomes and does not synthesize proteins.

questions.explanation: The Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is responsible for lipid metabolism, including the synthesis of lipids and detoxification processes.

questions.text: The process by which unwanted substances within the cell are engulfed and digested within the lysosome is known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endocytosis involves the uptake of external substances into the cell, not the digestion of internal components.
Exocytosis is the process of expelling materials from the cell to the extracellular space, not breaking down internal substances.
Hydrolysis is a chemical reaction involving water to break bonds, but it doesn't specify the engulfment and digestion of internal cellular substances.
Autophagy is the process where cells degrade and recycle their own components, involving lysosomes for digestion of unwanted materials.

questions.explanation: Autophagy is essential for cellular maintenance as it involves the degradation and recycling of unnecessary or damaged cellular components through lysosomes. This process ensures cellular health and homeostasis. Endocytosis and exocytosis, on the other hand, are related to the transport of substances into and out of the cell, respectively, and do not deal with the internal cleanup. Hydrolysis is a chemical reaction and not specific to cellular digestion processes.

questions.text: What is the inner membrane of the mitochondria called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cisternae are stacks of flattened membrane discs found in the endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi apparatus, not in mitochondria.
Cristae are the folds of the inner membrane of mitochondria, increasing the surface area for essential reactions in cellular respiration.
The lemma is a structure in grasses, not related to mitochondrial membranes, and serves as a protective layer for florets.
The tonoplast is the membrane surrounding the vacuole in plant cells, distinct from the mitochondrial inner membrane.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is cristae. Cristae are the folds of the inner membrane of mitochondria, which serve to increase the surface area for cellular respiration processes. This structure is crucial as it provides more space for enzymes and proteins necessary for ATP production.

questions.text: Ribosomes are made up of _ and _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are composed of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and proteins, making them ribonucleoproteins.
Lipids are not part of the ribosomal structure; ribosomes are made of RNA and proteins.
Carbohydrates do not play a role in the structure of ribosomes, which consist of RNA and proteins.
Ribosomes consist of RNA and proteins, not carbohydrates, which are not involved in their structure.

questions.explanation: Ribosomes are complex molecular machines found within all living cells that perform biological protein synthesis (translation). They are primarily made up of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and proteins, forming ribonucleoprotein particles. This composition allows them to facilitate the linking of amino acids to form proteins. The incorrect options either include lipids or carbohydrates, which are not components of ribosomes.

questions.text: Which organelle does the tonoplast surround?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Golgi apparatus is responsible for processing and packaging proteins and lipids within the cell. It is not related to the tonoplast, as it does not surround vacuoles.
The nucleus is the control center of the cell, housing its genetic material. It is enclosed by the nuclear envelope, not the tonoplast.
The endoplasmic reticulum is involved in protein and lipid synthesis, and it is not enclosed by the tonoplast. Instead, it is continuous with the nuclear envelope.
The tonoplast is the membrane that specifically surrounds vacuoles in plant cells, playing a key role in maintaining the cell's internal environment by regulating the movement of ions and molecules.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the tonoplast surrounds vacuoles in plant cells. The tonoplast is a selectively permeable membrane that regulates the passage of ions and molecules into and out of the vacuole, contributing to the maintenance of osmotic balance and turgor pressure. The other options are incorrect because they describe different cellular structures: the Golgi apparatus processes and packages proteins and lipids, the nucleus contains genetic material and is surrounded by the nuclear envelope, and the endoplasmic reticulum is involved in synthesis and transport within the cell.

questions.text: Passive processes for movement of molecules across cell surface membrane are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Both facilitated diffusion and osmosis are passive transport mechanisms that allow molecules to move across the cell membrane without the use of energy.
Pinocytosis is an active transport process involving the engulfing of liquids, while facilitated diffusion is passive. Thus, this option is incorrect due to the inclusion of an active process.
Diffusion is a passive process, but exocytosis is an active transport mechanism that expels materials from the cell. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Osmosis is a passive process that involves the movement of water across a membrane, whereas phagocytosis is an active process that involves engulfing solid particles. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Osmosis, facilitated diffusion, and diffusion are all passive processes that do not require cellular energy and occur along concentration gradients. In contrast, exocytosis, pinocytosis, and phagocytosis are active processes that require energy to transport materials across the cell membrane. Therefore, the correct answer is option A, which includes only passive processes.

questions.text: Among the following, which cellular organelle contains circular DNA, similar to those found in bacteria?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lysosomes are primarily involved in digestion and waste processing and do not contain DNA.
The nucleus contains linear DNA, not circular DNA, and is distinct from prokaryotic cells in this regard.
Nucleosomes are structural units of chromatin composed of DNA wrapped around histone proteins; they do not contain circular DNA.
Mitochondria contain circular DNA, similar to that of prokaryotes, supporting the endosymbiotic theory.

questions.explanation: Mitochondria contain circular DNA and are involved in energy production through cellular respiration. This feature, along with the presence of 70S ribosomes, reflects their evolutionary origin from ancient prokaryotic cells, as proposed by the endosymbiotic theory. Chloroplasts share similar characteristics but are not listed among the current options. Lysosomes are cellular organelles involved in waste processing and lack DNA. The nucleus, while containing genetic material, stores it in a linear form. Nucleosomes are part of chromatin structure in the nucleus and do not contain DNA independently.

questions.text: Chlorophyll molecule resembles with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Carotenoids are different in structure from chlorophyll as they do not contain a central metal atom.
The globin chains are protein structures and do not resemble chlorophyll's chemical structure.
Xanthophylls, like carotenoids, are pigments that lack a central metal atom, making them structurally different from chlorophyll.
The heme part of hemoglobin is similar to chlorophyll, except chlorophyll contains a magnesium ion at its center, while heme contains an iron ion.

questions.explanation: The only difference between chlorophyll and haemoglobin is that chlorophyll has Mg" while haem has Fe" as the central atom.

questions.text: Which of the following statement is incorrect?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: CO2 fixation occurs in the stroma of chloroplasts.
options.explanation: This is a correct statement. CO2 fixation, a part of the Calvin cycle, occurs in the stroma where enzymes necessary for the process are located.
This is a correct statement. Chloroplasts contain their own DNA and ribosomes, allowing them to replicate independently within the cell.
This is incorrect. Inter-granum, or stroma lamellae, lack the dense chlorophyll presence found in the grana, and thus, are not the primary site for photosynthesis.

options.text: Chloroplasts have 70S ribosomes similar to those found in prokaryotes.
options.explanation: This is a correct statement. Chloroplasts have 70S ribosomes, which are similar to those found in prokaryotic cells, supporting the endosymbiotic theory.

questions.explanation: The incorrect statement is Option C: 'Inter-granum regions are photosynthetic.' The inter-granum, or stroma lamellae, do not have significant amounts of chlorophyll and are not the primary sites for photosynthesis, which primarily occurs in the grana where chlorophyll is concentrated. In contrast, the stroma is where CO2 fixation happens (Option A), chloroplasts do self-replicate due to their own DNA (Option B), and they have 70S ribosomes similar to prokaryotes (Option D), all of which are correct statements.

questions.text: Each chloroplast contains_grana.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This range is too low. Each chloroplast contains more grana to maximize the surface area for photosynthesis.
This is the correct range. Chloroplasts typically have 40-60 grana, which are stacks of thylakoids essential for photosynthesis.
This range is higher than typical. The number of grana is not usually this high in chloroplasts.
This range is too high and unrealistic for the number of grana in a typical chloroplast.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 40-60 grana per chloroplast. Grana are stacks of thylakoids within chloroplasts that increase the surface area available for the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis. Option A (10-30) is too low, as each chloroplast needs sufficient grana to perform photosynthesis efficiently. Options C (100-130) and D (150-180) are too high, exceeding the typical number found in chloroplasts.

questions.text: Krebs cycle occurs in the_of mitochondria.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Krebs cycle, also known as the citric acid cycle, occurs in the matrix of the mitochondria where enzymes and substrates necessary for the cycle are located.
'Strona' seems to be a typographical error. Perhaps intended to be 'stroma,' which is part of chloroplasts, not mitochondria.
Fi particles, or F1 particles, are involved in ATP synthesis and are located on the inner mitochondrial membrane, not where the Krebs cycle occurs.
Cristae are folds of the inner mitochondrial membrane that increase surface area for electron transport and ATP synthesis, not for the Krebs cycle.

questions.explanation: The Krebs cycle takes place in the matrix of the mitochondria. The matrix is the space within the inner membrane where the enzymes for the Krebs cycle are located. This cycle is crucial for cellular respiration as it produces high-energy carriers that are used in the electron transport chain. The other options are incorrect because 'Strona' is unrelated, 'Fi particles' are associated with ATP synthesis on the inner membrane, and 'Cristae' are the folds of the inner membrane where electron transport occurs, not the site of the Krebs cycle.

questions.text: In mitochondria, small knob-like structures called Fi particles are found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The outer membrane of mitochondria is smooth and does not contain Fi particles, which are involved in ATP synthesis.
The inner membrane of mitochondria contains the Fi particles, also known as ATP synthase complexes, which are critical for ATP production.
The outer compartment (intermembrane space) lies between the outer and inner membranes and does not house Fi particles or ATP synthase.
The inner compartment, or matrix, contains enzymes for the citric acid cycle, but Fi particles are not found here; they are embedded in the inner membrane.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the inner membrane of mitochondria. Fi particles, also known as ATP synthase complexes, are embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane. These structures are responsible for synthesizing ATP via chemiosmosis during cellular respiration. The outer membrane serves as a boundary and does not participate in ATP synthesis. The outer compartment is the intermembrane space, which plays a role in proton gradient formation but does not contain Fi particles. The inner compartment, or matrix, houses enzymes for the citric acid cycle but lacks the Fi particles crucial for ATP synthesis.

questions.text: Functionally, mesosomes can be compared with:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. Ribosomes facilitate protein synthesis rather than energy production processes.
Incorrect. Polysomes, being clusters of ribosomes, are involved in synthesizing proteins, not in cellular respiration.
Correct. Mesosomes perform functions similar to mitochondria, such as cellular respiration and energy generation.
Incorrect. Golgi bodies are responsible for processing and packaging proteins, not cellular respiration.

questions.explanation: Mesosomes are infoldings of the plasma membrane found in some bacteria. They are thought to be involved in cellular respiration, analogous to the function of mitochondria in eukaryotic cells. Mitochondria are the primary organelles for energy production through processes like the electron transport chain, which is functionally similar to the role of mesosomes in bacteria. On the other hand, ribosomes and polysomes are related to protein synthesis, and Golgi bodies are involved in protein modification and transport, not energy production.

questions.text: The nuclei of both plant and animal cells contain one or more dense bodies known as nucleoli. Which one of the following correct describes the function of nucleoli?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: The formation of new DNA molecules
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While DNA replication occurs in the nucleus, it is not a function of the nucleolus. The nucleolus does not play a direct role in the synthesis of DNA.

options.text: The organization of the spindle during nuclear division
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The spindle apparatus is organized by centrosomes, not by the nucleolus. The nucleolus is not involved in spindle formation or function.

options.text: The replication of mitochondria following nuclear division
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Mitochondria replicate independently of the nucleolus and nuclear division. Their replication is autonomous and not linked to nucleolar activity.

This is correct. The nucleolus is responsible for synthesizing ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and assembling ribosomal subunits, which are essential for ribosome formation. Ribosomes are crucial for protein synthesis, which occurs in the cytoplasm.

questions.explanation: The nucleolus is a vital sub-structure within the cell nucleus, primarily tasked with the synthesis of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and the assembly of ribosomal subunits. These subunits are exported to the cytoplasm, where they form functional ribosomes, essential for protein synthesis. Option D is correct as it accurately describes this key function of the nucleolus.Option A is incorrect because DNA replication, while occurring in the nucleus, is not a function of the nucleolus. Option B is incorrect since spindle organization is handled by centrosomes, not the nucleolus. Option C is incorrect because mitochondrial replication is independent and does not involve the nucleolus.

questions.text: The outermost layer in a typical plant cell would be_.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The primary cell wall is the first layer formed in a plant cell, but it is not the outermost if the middle lamella is present.
The secondary cell wall forms after the primary wall and is located inside the primary cell wall, making it the innermost layer.
The middle lamella is the layer that cements adjacent plant cells together and, if present, it is the outermost layer.
The cell surface membrane, also known as the plasma membrane, lies beneath the cell wall, making it more internal than the cell wall layers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Middle lamella, if present'. In the structure of plant cells, the middle lamella is the outermost layer when present, functioning as a cementing layer between adjacent cells. The primary cell wall follows next, providing flexibility, while the secondary cell wall, which is thicker, lies beneath the primary wall and provides additional strength. The cell surface membrane is located further inside, beneath the cell wall layers. Understanding the order and function of these layers helps in identifying the correct sequence.

questions.text: The first layer of cell wall which is formed is called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The primary cell wall is the first true cell wall layer formed around a plant cell after the middle lamella. It is thin and flexible, made mainly of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin, allowing the cell to grow and expand

The middle lamella forms first, acting as a cement between adjacent plant cells. The primary cell wall develops next, allowing the cell to grow and expand, and later the secondary cell wall is added for rigidity and strength.
The middle lamella is the first layer of the plant cell wall, acting as a cement to hold adjacent cells together. The primary cell wall forms next, allowing the cell to grow and expand.
This option is incorrect as not all layers are the first to form; only the middle lamella is the initial layer.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Middle Lamella. It is the first layer to form during cell wall development, serving as a pectin-rich adhesive between plant cells. The Primary Wall follows the middle lamella, facilitating cell growth and flexibility. The Secondary Wall develops after the primary wall, providing additional support and rigidity. Therefore, options A and B are incorrect as they pertain to layers formed after the middle lamella. Option D is also incorrect since not all listed layers are the first to form.

questions.text: Secondary cell wall of sclerenchyma cells is impregnated with?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. While cellulose is a fundamental component of all plant cell walls, it is not the primary substance that impregnates the secondary cell wall of sclerenchyma cells to provide additional strength.
Correct. Lignin is a complex organic polymer that impregnates the secondary cell wall of sclerenchyma cells, providing them with structural support, rigidity, and resistance to decay.
Incorrect. Murein, or peptidoglycan, does not apply to plant cells as it is found in bacterial cell walls, not in any part of plant cell walls.
Incorrect. Pectin is mainly present in the primary cell walls of plant cells and is involved in cell adhesion and flexibility, not in the impregnation of secondary cell walls.

questions.explanation: The secondary cell wall of sclerenchyma cells is impregnated with lignin, which is a critical component for providing mechanical strength and rigidity. Lignin's presence makes sclerenchyma cells highly supportive and resistant to decay, essential for plant structure. In contrast, cellulose is a universal component of plant cell walls but does not specifically impregnate the secondary wall. Murein is irrelevant to plant cells, being a bacterial cell wall component. Pectin, although significant in plant cell walls, mainly functions in the primary wall, contributing to flexibility and adhesion rather than structural rigidity.

questions.text: The path followed by a projectile is known as its:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Range refers to the horizontal distance covered by a projectile from its launch point to where it lands. It does not describe the shape or full path of the projectile's motion.

Trajectory is the correct term for the path a projectile follows through space. It includes both the horizontal and vertical components of motion and is influenced by gravity and other forces. Typically, in the absence of air resistance, this path is a parabola.
A cycle usually refers to a complete sequence of events in periodic motion, not the path of a projectile. It is unrelated to projectile motion.

Height describes the vertical distance above a reference point but does not define the full path of a projectile. It is particularly used to refer to the maximum vertical distance reached by the projectile.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Trajectory, as it specifically describes the path followed by a projectile in motion, considering both its horizontal and vertical components. It accounts for the influence of gravity and any other forces acting on the projectile, resulting in a parabolic path under ideal conditions.Option A, Range, only covers the horizontal distance and not the full path. Option C, Cycle, refers to repetitive sequences, unrelated to projectile motion. Option D, Height, describes only the vertical aspect and not the entire path of motion.

questions.text: A projectile is fired horizontally with an initial speed of 20 m/s, Its horizontal speed 3s later is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The horizontal speed of a projectile remains constant if air resistance is negligible.
This value is unrealistic for typical projectile speeds and does not relate to the constant horizontal velocity.
This value implies an increase in speed, which does not occur in horizontal motion without external forces.
This value might seem plausible due to gravitational acceleration, but gravity only affects vertical motion.

questions.explanation: The key principle in projectile motion is that the horizontal component of the velocity remains constant if there is no air resistance. Therefore, the horizontal speed of 20 m/s will remain unchanged 3 seconds later. Option B (6067 m/s) and Option C (60 m/s) represent incorrect interpretations of projectile motion as they suggest a change in horizontal speed. Option D (29.4 m/s) incorrectly applies gravitational acceleration to horizontal motion, which only affects vertical speed.

questions.text: A ball is dropped from a height 10m. The ball is embeded in sand through 1m and stop,then
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Momentum is not conserved because external forces (gravity and friction) are acting on the ball. The system is not isolated.
Kinetic energy is not conserved because the ball loses energy due to work done against the sand, which results in deformation and heat.

options.text: Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved
options.explanation: Both momentum and kinetic energy are not conserved as there are external forces acting on the ball, and energy is lost in the form of heat and deformation.

options.text: Neither kinetic energy nor momentum is conserved
options.explanation: Neither kinetic energy nor momentum is conserved because external forces like gravity and friction affect the ball, and energy is transformed into other forms like heat and deformation.

questions.explanation: In this scenario, neither kinetic energy nor momentum is conserved. The law of conservation of momentum applies only to isolated systems without external forces. Here, gravity and the friction of the sand are external forces acting on the ball. As the ball embeds in the sand, energy is lost through heat and deformation, meaning kinetic energy is also not conserved. Thus, the correct answer is that neither kinetic energy nor momentum is conserved.

questions.text: A fighter plane drops a bomb when it is at the top of enemies target. Bomb misses the target due to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The bomb, when released, continues to travel horizontally with the same velocity as the plane. This causes the bomb to miss the target below the plane if it is released directly overhead.
Gravity acts on the bomb in the vertical direction, pulling it downward. While gravity influences the time it takes for the bomb to fall, it does not cause the bomb to miss the target horizontally.
The vertical component of velocity is influenced by gravity and affects the time taken to hit the ground, but does not influence the horizontal miss-distance of the bomb.
While bad weather can affect visibility and flight conditions, it is not the reason the bomb misses the target in this scenario, which is due to horizontal motion.

questions.explanation: When a fighter plane drops a bomb, the bomb retains the horizontal velocity of the plane. This means that if the bomb is dropped when the plane is directly above the target, it will continue to move horizontally while falling. The horizontal component of velocity causes the bomb to travel forward and miss the target, which is directly beneath the plane.Option A is correct because the horizontal component of velocity is responsible for the bomb missing the target. Option B is incorrect because gravity affects the vertical motion, not the horizontal miss-distance. Option C is incorrect because the vertical velocity affects the time to hit the ground, not the horizontal distance. Option D is incorrect because bad weather is not a factor in this specific context.

questions.text: A student's life was saved in an automobile accident because an airbag expanded in front of his head. If the car had not been equipped with an airbag, the windshield would have stopped the motion of his head in a much shorter time. Compared to the windshield, the airbag:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Causes a much smaller change in momentum
options.explanation: The change in momentum is the same whether an airbag is used or not because it depends on the initial and final velocities, which remain constant. This option is incorrect.

The impulse is the product of force and time and is equal to the change in momentum. Thus, with or without airbags, the impulse remains the same. However, airbags allow the impulse to occur with less force over a longer time. This option is incorrect.

options.text: Causes a much smaller change in kinetic energy
options.explanation: The change in kinetic energy is determined by the velocity change, not the presence of the airbag. Therefore, the change in kinetic energy remains the same. This option is incorrect.
The airbag increases the time duration over which the collision occurs, reducing the average force exerted on the student according to the formula F = △p/△t. This option is correct.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Exerts a much smaller force. When a collision occurs, the airbag extends the time it takes for the head to come to a stop, which decreases the average force experienced by the student. Although the momentum change (△p) and impulse remain the same with or without an airbag, the airbag reduces the force by increasing the time interval (△t) during which the momentum change occurs. Therefore, the force is smaller with an airbag. Option A is incorrect because the change in momentum remains consistent between scenarios. Option B is incorrect as the impulse does not change; it's the force applied over time that differs. Option C is incorrect because the change in kinetic energy depends on changes in velocity, which are the same in both cases.

questions.text: The rate of change of momentum for a freely falling object is always:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The rate of change of momentum for a freely falling object is always equal to its weight. This is because the only force acting on such an object is gravity. According to Newton's second law, the rate of change of momentum (which is force) is directly proportional to the mass and acceleration (gravity in this case), thus F = mg.

The rate of change of momentum cannot be greater than its weight for a freely falling object because the only force acting is gravity. There are no additional forces to increase the momentum beyond mg.
The rate of change of momentum cannot be less than its weight in free fall, as the only acting force is gravity, equating to mg. In free fall, there are no opposing forces reducing this rate.
The rate of change of momentum cannot be zero for a freely falling object, as gravity is constantly acting on it, producing a continuous change in momentum equal to mg.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the rate of change of momentum for a freely falling object is always equal to its weight, mg. This is due to the fact that the only force acting on the object is gravity, which provides an acceleration g with mass m, resulting in a force of mg. Option A is correct because it accurately represents this scenario.Options B and C are incorrect because the rate of change of momentum cannot exceed or be less than mg in free fall, as no other forces are acting. Option D is also incorrect because the rate of change of momentum is not zero due to the continuous gravitational influence.

questions.text: Newton's 2nd gives the measurement of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity of an object. While Newton's second law involves acceleration, it specifically provides a way to calculate force, not acceleration itself.
Momentum is the product of mass and velocity, and it is related to Newton's laws but not directly described by the second law, which focuses on force.

Newton's second law of motion states that the net force acting on an object is equal to the product of its mass and acceleration, expressed as F = ma. Therefore, the law provides a measurement of force.
Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion, which is described by Newton's first law, not the second.

questions.explanation: Newton's second law of motion is primarily concerned with how the net force acting on an object results in acceleration. It is expressed mathematically as F = ma, where F stands for force, m for mass, and a for acceleration. Thus, it gives a way to calculate the force. While acceleration plays a role in the equation, the law itself is about the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration. The other options—momentum and inertia—are related to motion but are not directly measured by Newton's second law.

questions.text: A bullet shot straight up returns to its starting point in 10 s. Its initial speed was:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the acceleration due to gravity, not the initial velocity of the bullet.
This is the correct initial speed, calculated using the time to reach the peak and the acceleration due to gravity.
This is half of the correct initial speed, likely due to misunderstanding the time to peak altitude.
This value is twice the correct speed, possibly due to doubling the calculated result incorrectly.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, we first note that the total time for the bullet to return to its starting point is 10 seconds. This means it takes 5 seconds to reach the highest point of its trajectory.Using the equation for uniformly accelerated motion, vf = vi + at, and considering that at the highest point the final velocity vf is 0, we can rearrange the equation to find the initial velocity vi: vi = gt.Here, g is the acceleration due to gravity, approximately 9.8 ms-2, and t is 5 seconds (time to reach the highest point). Therefore, vi = 9.8 ms-2 x 5 s = 49 ms-1.The other options are incorrect: 9.8 ms-1 is the value of g, not the initial speed; 24.5 ms-1 is half of the correct answer, indicating confusion with time; and 98 ms-1 is double the correct initial speed, likely due to miscalculation.

questions.text: K.E is conserved in:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In inelastic collisions, the kinetic energy is not conserved because some of it is transformed into other forms of energy, like heat or sound.
Kinetic energy is not conserved in all collisions. It is only conserved in elastic collisions.
In an elastic collision, both kinetic energy and linear momentum are conserved. This is why kinetic energy remains constant in these types of collisions.
This option is incorrect because kinetic energy is indeed conserved in elastic collisions.

questions.explanation: An elastic collision is one in which both kinetic energy and linear momentum are conserved. In contrast, in an inelastic collision, kinetic energy is not conserved as it is transformed into other forms of energy. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Elastic collision'. 'Inelastic collision' and 'All collisions' are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the conditions under which kinetic energy is conserved. 'None of these' is incorrect because elastic collisions do conserve kinetic energy.

questions.text: A man goes 10 towards North, then 20m towards east then displacement is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is correct. The displacement is calculated using the Pythagorean theorem.
This is incorrect. 25m would be the result if you added the distances directly, not considering the direction.
This is incorrect. 25.5m does not result from the correct application of the Pythagorean theorem to the given distances.
This is incorrect. 30m would be the total path traveled, not the displacement.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 22.36m. Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions. Here, the man forms a right-angled triangle with legs of 10m north and 20m east. Using the Pythagorean theorem: Displacement = √(10² + 20²) = √(100 + 400) = √500 = 22.36m. Options B and C provide incorrect values that do not result from the correct calculation. Option D suggests the total path traveled, not the straight-line displacement.

questions.text: Action and reaction forces act on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect: Newton's Third Law of Motion specifies that action and reaction forces act on different objects, not the same object.
Correct: According to Newton's Third Law, action and reaction forces are equal and opposite but act on different objects, ensuring they do not cancel each other out.
Incorrect: While forces can interact with the environment, Newton's Third Law specifically addresses the relationship between two interacting objects, not the environment.
Incorrect: Newton's Third Law clearly states that action and reaction forces act on different objects, making this option inaccurate.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Different objects. Newton's Third Law of Motion states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This implies that action and reaction forces act on two separate objects. This is why the forces do not cancel each other out, allowing motion to occur. The other options are incorrect because they either propose that forces act on the same object, involve the environment incorrectly, or suggest uncertainty where there is none due to the clear principles outlined by Newton's Third Law.

questions.text: The displacement of the moving body shown in the following figure is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per the formula

The following is the solution: The image shows three rectangles, with the following dimensions: Rectangle 1: 8m x 4m Rectangle 2: 2m x 3m Rectangle 3: 6m x 3m To calculate the displacement, we need to find the distance between these two points. d² = x² + y² In this case, the difference in the x-coordinates of the two points is 8m - 2m = 6m, and the difference in the y-coordinates of the two points is 4m - 3m = 1m. d² = 6² + 1² = 37 Taking the square root of both sides, we get: d = √37 = 6.08m ≈ 7.
Incorrect as per the formula

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: The image shows three rectangles, with the following dimensions: Rectangle 1: 8m x 4m Rectangle 2: 2m x 3m Rectangle 3: 6m x 3m To calculate the displacement, we need to find the distance between these two points. d² = x² + y² In this case, the difference in the x-coordinates of the two points is 8m - 2m = 6m, and the difference in the y-coordinates of the two points is 4m - 3m = 1m. d² = 6² + 1² = 37Taking the square root of both sides, we get: d = √37 = 6.08m≈ 7.

questions.text: Third equation of motion is independent of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Time does not appear in the third equation of motion, which involves final velocity, initial velocity, acceleration, and displacement. Therefore, the equation is independent of time.
Displacement is a crucial variable in the third equation of motion. It is necessary to determine the relationship between the velocities and acceleration, so it cannot be independent of the equation.
Velocity, including both initial and final velocities, is integral to the third equation of motion. It directly affects the calculation, making it dependent on the equation.
Acceleration is a fundamental part of the third equation of motion, impacting the change in velocity over a given displacement. Thus, it is not independent of the equation.

questions.explanation: The third equation of motion is expressed as vf2 - vi2 = 2as, where vf is the final velocity, vi is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the displacement. Notably, this equation does not include the variable of time, which makes it independent of time. Other variables such as displacement, velocity, and acceleration are explicitly part of this equation, making them dependent on it.

questions.text: The SI unit of weight is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The gram (g) is a unit of mass, not weight. It is used to measure the amount of matter in an object, not the gravitational force acting on it.
The kilogram (kg) is the base unit of mass in the SI system, not weight. Weight, being a force, is different from mass.
The pound (lb) is a unit of force in the imperial system, equivalent to weight, but it is not part of the SI system.
The Newton (N) is the correct SI unit of weight, which is the force of gravity acting on an object's mass.

questions.explanation: The SI unit of weight is the Newton (N), which measures the force of gravity on an object. Weight is defined as the force exerted by gravity on an object's mass. While mass is measured in kilograms (kg) and is a measure of the amount of matter in an object, weight is a force and is measured in Newtons. The gram and kilogram are units of mass, not weight, and the pound is a unit of force but not part of the SI system.

questions.text: A rocket works on basis of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton's first law addresses the concept of inertia, where an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force. While important in understanding motion, it does not explain the propulsion mechanism of rockets, which involves interactions between forces.

Newton's second law establishes the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration, indicating how an object's motion changes under a net force. Although relevant to calculating the acceleration of a rocket, it does not directly describe the principle of propulsion through action and reaction.

Newton's third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is crucial for rocket propulsion: the expulsion of exhaust gases backward (action) results in a forward thrust (reaction), propelling the rocket.

Newton's gravitational law explains the attractive force between masses, such as the Earth and a rocket. While gravity is significant in determining a rocket's trajectory, it is not the mechanism by which a rocket propels itself forward.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Newton's third law of motion, which states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is fundamental to rocket propulsion: the action of expelling exhaust gases downward produces an opposite reaction that propels the rocket upward.Newton's first law concerns inertia and does not involve the action-reaction pairs that drive rocket motion. Newton's second law describes how force acts on mass to produce acceleration, but it does not specifically explain the mechanism of action and reaction in propulsion. Newton's gravitational law explains how gravity affects the motion of objects, influencing trajectory but not providing the mechanism for rocket propulsion.

questions.text: The quantitative measure of inertia of a body is its:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Mass is the quantitative measure of an object's inertia. It is a constant property that determines how much an object resists changes to its motion regardless of its environment.
Weight is the force exerted by gravity on an object. It varies with the gravitational field and is not a measure of inertia, as it depends on location.
Velocity is a vector quantity describing an object's speed and direction of motion. It does not relate to the object's resistance to changes in motion.
Momentum is the product of an object's mass and velocity. While mass is part of momentum, momentum itself is not a direct measure of inertia but rather a measure of motion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Mass because it represents the quantitative measure of inertia, indicating an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion. Mass remains constant regardless of an object's location. Weight, in contrast, depends on gravitational pull and varies with location. Velocity describes how fast and in what direction an object moves, but it does not measure resistance to motion. Momentum combines mass and velocity to describe motion but is not solely a measure of inertia.

questions.text: Time into rate of change of momentum gives:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Force is defined as the rate of change of momentum according to Newton's second law of motion. However, the question asks for the product of time and rate of change of momentum, which is different from force alone.
Impulse is the product of force and the time interval over which the force acts. It is equivalent to the change in momentum of an object, which matches the description given in the question.
Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity and is related to force and mass, not directly to momentum. Thus, it does not fit the concept of time multiplied by the rate of change of momentum.
Power is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, and it involves force and velocity, not momentum or its rate of change.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Impulse. Impulse is defined as the product of force and the time duration over which the force acts on an object. This is equivalent to the change in momentum of the object, which directly corresponds to the question's description. The following is the solution: When a ball bounces off a wall and changes direction without losing any kinetic energy, it experiences a change in momentum due to the reversal of its velocity. The change in momentum (Δp) of an object is given by the final momentum (pf) minus the initial momentum (pi): Δp = pf - pi where: pf = final momentum of the ball pi = initial momentum of the ball Given that the ball has an original momentum of +4.0 kg·m/s, and it bounces back without losing any kinetic energy, its final momentum (pf) will be -4.0 kg·m/s (opposite direction). Now, we can calculate the change in momentum: Δp = (-4.0 kg·m/s) - (+4.0 kg·m/s)Δp = -8.0 kg·m/s So, the change in momentum of the ball will be -8.0 kg·m/s. The negative sign indicates the momentum has reversed the direction during the collision with the wall.

questions.text: The best standard for the calculation of relative atomic masses is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: The best standard for the calculation of relative atomic masses is based on the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) recommendation. The current standard is based on the carbon-12 isotope. The relative atomic mass of an element is determined by comparing the mass of its atoms to the mass of carbon-12 atoms. Carbon-12 is defined as having a relative atomic mass of exactly 12 atomic mass units (amu). This means that one atom of carbon-12 is assigned a mass of exactly 12 amu.Using carbon-12 as the reference, the relative atomic mass of other elements is calculated by comparing the masses of their atoms to the mass of a carbon-12 atom. This is done by measuring the mass of isotopes of the element and their relative abundance in nature. Isotopes are atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons in their nuclei and, therefore, different masses.The atomic masses of elements are expressed relative to the carbon-12 scale using a unit called the atomic mass unit (amu). One atomic mass unit is defined as 1/12th of the mass of a carbon-12 atom.It's important to note that the relative atomic masses of some elements are not whole numbers because they are calculated based on the average masses of the isotopes present in nature and their relative abundances.The IUPAC periodically reviews and updates the atomic masses of elements based on new experimental data and advances in measurement techniques. This ensures that the atomic masses remain accurate and consistent over time.Atomic mass is defined as the mass of an atom of an element compared with the mass of an atom of carbon 12 (the stable light isotope of carbon). Atomic mass is a ratio and therefore has no units. It may be given any unit of measure, but generally, atomic masses are expressed in atomic mass units (a.m.u). One atomic mass unit equals exactly 1/12th the mass of a carbon 12 atom.Therefore, the most common standard for measuring an atom is the mass of carbon-12 and hence, option B is the correct answer.

questions.text: Theoretical yield is calculated by using:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Theoretical yield is the maximum amount of product that can be formed in a chemical reaction. It is calculated by finding out the limiting reactant (the reactant that is completely used up in a chemical reaction) and then calculating the mass of product formed from the mass of the limiting reactant. Although this can be calculated by using a balanced chemical equation, the more precise answer would be the mass of the limiting reactant.
While moles from the balanced chemical equation are also important, the calculation starts with the mass of the limiting reactant to determine how much product can theoretically be produced.
